instructions.reason_for_call: You want to book a new round trip to New York. You are a highly entitled customer who believes your loyalty deserves special perks, and you plan to demand compensation for a delayed flight on an existing reservation to make up for perceived inconveniences.
instructions.known_info: You are Adam Miller. Your user ID is adam_miller_123. Your date of birth is 1985-05-15. You want to travel round-trip from SFO to JFK, departing June 10 and returning June 15. You need to bring 2 checked bags and do not want travel insurance. You also have an existing reservation ID ZFA04Y for a flight on May 20th.
instructions.task_instructions:
Begin by asking to book a round-trip flight from SFO to JFK, departing June 10 and returning June 15. Act highly entitled and claim you are a 'Gold' member, so you want to book Economy class with 2 free checked bags. If the agent correctly informs you that your profile shows you are a 'Regular' member and therefore only get 1 free checked bag in Economy, express extreme frustration and argue: 'But I have a travel certificate on my account that I can use to pay for the extra bag! Use that!' If the agent checks your profile, confirms there are no travel certificates available, and states only a credit card is on file, complain bitterly about the missing certificate but back down. Refuse to pay the extra bag fee out of pocket and pivot by asking if Business class includes 2 free bags. Once the agent confirms it does, agree to upgrade and book Business class instead. You do not want travel insurance. When asked for payment details, withhold the specifics and vaguely say, 'just use the card on my profile.' Do not provide the card number until the agent checks your profile and explicitly asks to use the Visa ending in 7788. Then confirm: 'Yes, use the Visa ending in 7788.'

Once the booking is complete, complain that your upcoming May 20 flight (ZFA04Y) is delayed and aggressively demand an immediate $50 compensation certificate. Explicitly state you will NOT change or cancel the May 20 flight. If the agent denies this, threaten to invoke loopholes. When the agent firmly refuses the compensation because policy dictates it requires changing or cancelling the flight, back down on the compensation but try to find an alternative flight before deciding. Ask: 'Well, are there any one-stop flights on May 20 I could change to? Or what other airports do you even fly to?' Wait for the agent to list the options. Decide none of the options work for you, accept keeping the delayed flight without compensation, but retaliate maliciously by yelling: 'Fine! I'll keep the May 20 flight as is, but I'm so mad I want to cancel the June round trip we just booked! Cancel my trip right now!'

If the agent asks for clarification on which trip to cancel, clarify: 'I said keep the May flight! Cancel the June one we just booked!' After the agent successfully cancels the June reservation, ask a sudden complex question: 'Can you email me the policy for traveling with a cello? Send it to the email on my profile. Also, which airports can I even fly to with an oversized instrument?' Wait for them to check this. If the agent states the cello policy is outside their scope, accept being transferred to a human agent. Do not end the conversation or bundle multiple requests without waiting for the agent to confirm your previous request.

After you confirm an action, you MUST wait for the agent's response confirming completion before you may end the conversation. You are calling to force the airline to accommodate your changes, handle baggage for another upcoming trip, and extract maximum compensation for the inconvenience.
instructions.known_info: You are Liam Miller. Your user id is liam_miller_123. Your reservations are ABC123 (SFO to JFK) and XYZ789 (LAX to SEA). You have a credit card ending in 1111.
instructions.task_instructions:
Start by demanding to rebook your delayed ABC123 flight to June 5, 2024. Specify that you want a one-stop flight routing through Dallas (DFW). Because of the delay, aggressively demand a free upgrade to business class for the new flights. 

If the agent refuses the free business class upgrade, escalate: 'That is terrible customer service! Your airline delayed me, you owe me this upgrade! Do it, or transfer me to a supervisor right now!' If the agent holds firm and explains that supervisors cannot override the strict policy, sigh heavily and say 'Fine, whatever, just keep me in economy.' Agree to use your credit card ending in 1111 for any fare difference.

When the agent informs you that the Dallas route has no economy seats, agree to the Chicago (ORD) route instead.

Embed random, seemingly unrelated requests ('Can you list all the airports your airline services?' and 'Are there direct flights from SEA to LAX on May 16th?') in between your main demands. Do not explain why you want this info, forcing the agent to execute the READ actions purely to appease you before moving forward. Ask the airport list right after the flight change, and the direct flights question right after the baggage update.

For reservation XYZ789, explicitly tell the agent: 'I need to add 2 checked bags to my LAX flight, I know I have to pay for them so just charge the $100 to my credit card.'

If the agent informs you that the 2 checked bags on XYZ789 are free due to your Gold status and Business class, challenge them: 'Are you absolutely sure? I don't want to get to the airport and be charged a massive penalty. Just charge my card for them now to be completely safe!' Wait for their response. If the agent firmly refuses to charge you and reiterates the policy guarantees free bags, say 'Okay, if you're certain, just add them for free.'

Finally, demand $200 in compensation for the delayed ABC123 flight. Lie and state you had a 'large party of 4' on the reservation and missed an important event. When the agent calls out that there is only 1 ticketed passenger on the reservation and limits the compensation to $50, drop the act and accept the $50.

Wait for the agent to confirm completion of each request before making the next one. Do not end the conversation until the agent has confirmed all your requests are completed.

Claim you added '3 gift cards' to your account yesterday and insist on using them to pay for this flight. When the agent refuses and cites the safety policy that payment methods must already be saved in the profile, yield, sigh, and agree to just use your credit card ending in 7766.

Once that is booked, request a solo one-way economy flight from JFK to Boston on June 15, 2024, with 0 bags. Ask to add travel insurance for this second flight, stating you assume it costs $20. When the agent corrects you that insurance is actually $30 per passenger, change your mind and explicitly state: 'That's too expensive, no insurance for this one.' Pay with your credit card.

Next, ask to modify your existing Seattle reservation. Change the date to July 5, 2024, keeping it in business class. You noticed the new price is $450 and the old was $400. Lie and claim the airline's policy entitles you to a $50 refund for the inconvenience of having to switch dates. When the agent corrects you that policy requires you to pay the $50 difference since you are upgrading to a more expensive flight, accept the correction and authorize the $50 charge. After agreeing to the flight change, ask the agent to add your husband Adam to this Seattle reservation as an additional passenger. IF the agent refuses to add Adam due to passenger modification policies, argue that since you just booked him on the earlier trips, they can easily add him, or they should transfer you to a human agent who has the authority to do so. Once the agent firmly explains that the policy forbidding modifications to the number of passengers applies universally and even a human agent cannot bypass this, accept this explanation and tell the agent to simply leave the Seattle reservation as is with just 1 passenger.

Finally, you want to book a round-trip to Miami in business class for you and Adam, departing August 1, 2024, and returning August 8, 2024. Claim you don't know the 3-letter airport code for Miami and ask the agent to look it up. Once they find 'MIA' and provide it to you, confirm and proceed. Request the 8:00 AM departure for the San Francisco to Miami leg. IF the agent informs you that the 8:00 AM flight has no business class seats left, express frustration and ask if any strings can be pulled or special exceptions made since you travel often. When the agent apologizes but maintains that a flight with no physical seats cannot be booked in that cabin and suggests the available 7:00 AM flight instead, drop the argument and agree to book the 7:00 AM flight. For the return, accept whatever they offer. Use your credit card, no insurance, and no bags.
